notes: Ledger for skillminer. Every nightly scan mutates `candidates[]`, replaces `observations[]`, sets `last_scan`. `rejected[]` and `deferred[]` block re-proposal for 30 days via semantic (not just slug) match — entries REQUIRE `intentSummary` and `triggerPhrases`. `silenced[]` blocks re-proposal permanently (no expiry). `observations[]` is scan-scoped — fully replaced each scan with the current scan's sub-threshold patterns; a human can run `manage-ledger.sh promote <id>` to pull one into `candidates[]` as pending. Preferred mutation path: `scripts/manage-ledger.sh accept|reject|defer|promote|silence|unsilence <id> [reason]` — deterministic, atomic, copies required fields. Hand-editing state.json also works: set `status` on the candidate to accepted/rejected/deferred and the scan prompt (Step 3) + morning-write sweep both respect the new state.

candidate.intentSummary: one-sentence description of the underlying task (used for semantic cooldown matching)
candidate.occurrences: integer — COUNT IN THE MOST RECENT SCAN WINDOW (not cumulative lifetime)
candidate.confidence: high|medium|low — set at initial proposal, never mutated by later scans
candidate.coverageRisk: true when a capability-overlapping skill exists but no trigger-level duplicate
candidate.rejectedReason: null | string — populated when human hand-edits status=rejected instead of using manage-ledger.sh reject
candidate.rejectedAt: null | YYYY-MM-DD — populated when human hand-edits status=rejected
candidate.deferredReason: null | string — populated when human hand-edits status=deferred
candidate.deferredAt: null | YYYY-MM-DD — populated when human hand-edits status=deferred
candidate.resurfacedFrom: null | 'rejected' | 'deferred' | 'observation' — populated when a cooldown-expired entry re-qualifies OR promote lifted it from observations[]
candidate.resurfacedFromDate: null | YYYY-MM-DD — original decision date when resurfaced, or promotion date for observation promotes
candidate.promotedFromObservation: bool — true when created via `manage-ledger.sh promote`; proposedSteps may be empty

observation.occurrences: integer — count in current scan window
observation.previousOccurrences: null | integer — prior scan's observation count for the same semantic pattern; null means new or legacy data without trend history
observation.previousDays: null | integer — prior scan's distinct-day count for the same semantic pattern; null means new or legacy data without trend history
observation.proposedSteps: optional — may be empty; scan is not required to infer steps for sub-threshold patterns
observation.reason: why this did not qualify (e.g. 'only 2 occurrences, watch another week')
observation._note: observations[] is fully REPLACED each scan. Do not hand-edit for persistence — run `manage-ledger.sh promote <id>` or `silence <id>` instead. Existing 0.3 observations may lack previousOccurrences/previousDays; the scan should treat missing values as null, not as zero.

rejected.intentSummary: one-sentence description — REQUIRED for semantic cooldown matching
triggerPhrases: REQUIRED — copied from the original candidate so future scans can detect semantically-equivalent resurfaces

deferred.intentSummary: one-sentence description — REQUIRED for semantic cooldown matching

silenced.intentSummary: one-sentence description — REQUIRED for semantic silence matching
triggerPhrases: REQUIRED — copied from the source so future scans detect semantic resurfaces and continue to skip them
silenced._note: No expiry. A silenced id is never proposed again as candidate OR observation until `unsilence` is run.
